codeReview.summary: The feature implementation covers the required matching and settlement behaviors: price-time priority matching, full/partial fills, self-trade skipping, maker-price execution, and quote/base transfer settlement with the expected fixed-point formula. Tests added in this commit exercise the requested scenarios and align with VAL-CONTRACT-014 through VAL-CONTRACT-018.

issues.description: FOK orders can degrade into partial-fill behavior in a self-trade edge case: after precheck, if matching leaves remainder (e.g., self orders are skipped), the code unlocks remaining funds instead of aborting as strict fill-or-kill semantics would require. This does not block the scoped matching/settlement acceptance criteria but is a semantic edge case.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The move-worker skill says to write tests first (red phase), but this run implemented core modules and integration edits before adding new matching/settlement tests; handoff still reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl (session e0063927-6950-4b35-b7f5-ad643b7fb745) shows module/file implementation and compile/test passes before the step labeled "Now I need to write comprehensive tests".

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state contains mixed guidance on market scope, which can affect review and implementation decisions for pair-scoped matching behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md states trading pairs should support multiple base/quote pairs, while .factory/library/architecture.md defines this mission as a single-market CASH/USDC system.

summary: Reviewed commit 82382ac and the assigned transcript/handoff evidence for contract-matching-settlement. Result is pass for scoped feature requirements, with one non-blocking FOK edge-case note and shared-state observations on skill-process drift and market-scope documentation inconsistency.
